desc:
The stream flows steadily as it makes its way towards the Celebrant. Golden
mallorn blooms occasionally float down from the great trees above to become as
little boats on a journey through Lothlorien. Some gather along the edge of a
green, mossy stone, but the current quickly dislodges them and sends them on
their way.
name: Stream Flowing toward Celebrant

desc:
The stream is wide and fairly deep here. It would be possible to dip a
shortsword into the water at the midpoint between the two banks and still not
touch the streambed below. The clear water is as cold, as if it was all runoff
from the ice-melts in the mountains to the west.
name: Wide Stream

desc:
The stream begins to widen as it leaves the higher lands and heads farther down
toward the main arm of the Celebrant. The banks of the stream are laden with
flat mossy stones that give way to lush green grasses that seem steeped in dew.
The water of the stream itself is clear and bright, allowing the pebbles on the
bottom of the stream to be seen as they reflect the golden light from above.
name: Widening Stream

desc:
The stream cascades down a waterfall to the west and gathers into a small pool
here. The water falls about five feet over slick mossy rocks, making travel to
the west difficult to attempt. Large rocks covered with thick green algae
dominate the bottom of the pool. It would be easy to slip and fall head first
into the chilly waters.
name: A Small Pool

desc:
A small stream exits from under a low wooden bridge to the north and turns east,
where the water cascades over a small waterfall and gathers in a pool below.
Many large flat-topped rocks are embedded into the streambed, making it easy to
traverse the stream.
name: Atop a Small Waterfall

desc:
A huge rent in the forest floor opens to the east. The forest floor drops off
sharply, making travel that way impossible. It looks as if some massive blade
sliced the very earth itself, leaving a deep cut that is slowly being filled up
with old leaves and golden blooms.
name: West of a Long Crack

desc:
The golden forest unfolds its beauty for all to see. The tall, silvery grey
mellyrn stand in lines in this part of the forest, creating a corridor from
north to south. Their blossom-laden or leaf-laden boughs dip and sway, making
their leaves or golden flowers dance in the breeze. On the forest floor, flowers
of light purple, pale yellow and bright white grow in abundance.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
All around, the immense mallorn trees heave their silvery trunks toward the
heavens. During the day, the golden tree-crowns filter the sunlight from above,
casting warm golden hues over everything that dwells in this peaceful wood. The
yellow elanor and pale niphredil flowers are abundant here, dotting the lush
grasses and growing in clusters at the base of the mighty mellyrn.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
This narrow trail is lined with majestic mellyrn, their great boughs spread wide
overhead, creating a canopy. Occasionally a golden flower floats down from above
and glides softly to the moist green turf. The turf is spotted with holes and
divots; apparently a family of squirrels or other small animals has been storing
food here.
name: Peaceful Trail

desc:
Covered with green grass, the forest trail runs from the west to the east. The
sound of running water comes from somewhere nearby. Mallorn trees seem to guide
the path; to the north the majestic silvery trunks block passage, but the Golden
Wood can be entered to the south.
name: Peaceful Trail

desc:
This small bridge offers a vantage point over the area. The Golden Wood is all
about you; mellyrn of various heights raise their stout, silvery trunks high to
the canopy above. Laden by foliage and blossoms, the branches sway slowly from
side to side as a gentle wind blows through, bringing sweet smells to your nose
and lilting notes of the forest to your ears.
name: Bridge over a Stream

desc:
The forest grows dense here. The great silvery arms of the mellyrn intertwine to
create a patchwork of green and pale golden hues that mask the view above after
only ten feet or so. Soft winds whisper through the trees. Occasionally the
melodic tune of the rustling mallorn boughs is punctuated by a bird call. Barely
visible among the grey trunks, a silvery stripe hangs from one of the larger
mallorn-trees.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The green grass here is reminiscent of freshly fallen snow because it looks as
if no living being has ever tread upon it. The canopy of leaves and branches
conceals the sky, yet the light from above is still able to make its way down
through the mallorn boughs in bright slants.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
A deep gash in the ground opens to the west; the drop is sharp and steep. A
mighty event must have caused such a huge opening to form in the floor of the
Golden Wood itself. A solitary mallorn-tree overlooks this ravine, its great
boughs sagging over the depths below.
name: East of a Long Crack

desc:
The trees and undergrowth get thinner to the east where a stream passes through
the forest. Running from the north to the south, it grows steadily ever wider,
as it makes its way towards the Celebrant. The rushing water has cut deeply into
the streambed, making it impossible to get down to the stream from the
tree-covered bank.
name: Beside a Stream

desc:
Thick, lush grass grows to two feet and higher on this small plot of land that
is wedged between a small pool to the north and a running stream to the east.
This seems like the perfect place for a tired traveller to lie down and gaze up
at the graceful interwoven boughs of mellyrn adorned with flowers and leaves of
gold or pale green.
name: Moist Grass

desc:
The mallorn-trees here are neither very young, nor too tall; most likely, the
soft soil near the stream does not allow for deep roots, limiting their growth.
The shorter trees allow more light through to the ground and more flowers to
grow among the tree-trunks. The golden and white elanor and niphredil here are
joined by various flowers of pink and soft blues.
name: Mallorn Copse

desc:
The mallorn-trees stand in a circle here. Their limbs stretch towards the centre
of the circle in a manner that makes this clearing look like a temple or a
gathering place. One could imagine a festival or wedding being held here, among
the silvery-grey mallorn trunks standing tall like the pillars of a grand
building.
name: Circle of Trees

desc:
The trail of well-trodden grass leads up to a stream to the west. One can hear
the soft gurgling of water as it makes its way over the rocks in the stream. A
light, airy scent of flowers and fresh water is in the air. The mellyrn stand
tall, soaring seemingly straight to the heavens. Their silvery trunks block the
way to the north and south, making it impossible to enter the Golden Wood
proper. The path leads away from the stream eastwards.
name: Trail beside a Stream

desc:
Many flowers speckle the sides of this forest trail as it makes its way along a
stream. Tiny yellow flowers, named elanor by the elves, along with the pale
blooms of the niphredil, poke out from the lush green grass. Their faint aroma
barely reaches your nose.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
This shaded part of the forest offers some comfort for those weary from life on
the road. The silvery-trunked trees have a light mossy coating that is soft and
cool to the touch. The plush green grass is dotted with the fragile pale yellow
mallorn blooms that have dropped from above. A little hole in the ground at the
base of a nearby mallorn shows evidence that a family of chipmunks or other
small forest creatures lives here.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The trail begins to wind its way through the forest, a small stream ambling
along in parallel with it, at some distance to the west. The sound of running
water gurgling down the streambed as it makes its course towards the main river
can clearly be heard from this spot.
name: Winding Trail

desc:
The trail bends here to avoid the stream which is beginning to pick up its pace
as it heads from the north to join to the main arm of the mighty Celebrant to
the south. The trail continues on to the north and to the east, its turf soft
and springy - not the hard matted dirt of most forest trails.
name: Trail through the Golden Wood

desc:
The majesty of the Golden Wood continues to radiate, as the heart of this
seemingly undying land is revealed for all to see. When light of the skies falls
from above, the pale yellow and gold hues of the mallorn crowns cast a warm glow
on the surroundings. A couple of mallorn nuts have fallen at the base of one of
the trees.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The golden-laden mellyrn boughs open up a little bit here. The fresh scents of
grasses and flowers fill the air. To the east the trees fall away completely,
revealing a clearing in the woods, in the heart of the Naith. The lack of trees
ahead allows the sunlight to cascade on this area during the day, and at night
the moonlight adds a silvery tint.
name: Near a Clearing

desc:
This part of the Golden Wood continues to display the treasures of Lothlorien.
The forest floor is covered in the remnants of golden blooms which have fallen
from the canopy above during previous years. Were these blooms gathered into a
bed, they might make the most comfortable resting place ever.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The trail makes a sharp turn here and heads both south and west. Sounds of the
forest glide to your ear. You can hear the creaking of the trees, the rustle of
the leaves, lilting animal cries in the distance and countless other sounds
that, added together, seem a symphony.
name: Sharp Turn in the Winding Trail

desc:
The trail here is a straight passage from north to south. The grass is very wet,
especially on the west side of trail, kept moist by the forest stream. The
flowers near the stream grow tall and their scent seems almost to be
overpowering. Strange mosses also grow near the stream; their golden-greenish
hue matches the mallorn foliage nearly identically.
name: Straightaway

desc:
An elven trail through the Golden Wood passes directly to the west of here, its
well-trodden turf easily noticeable against the longer grass. The grass grows
especially thick and deep in this part of the forest that is nearly surrounded
by the waters of the Celebrant and its tributaries. All around the mellyrn rise,
intermingled with birch and elm trees.
name: Off the Trail

desc:
The sounds of the forest echo in your ears. The rushing water of the stream can
be heard to the west, but it is by far not as noisy as it would be were one to
stand right at the stream bank. The noise from a distance somehow makes this
part of the forest seem all the more peaceful by the contrast it affords.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The silvery bark on the trees glitters faintly, making images of an ancient past
dance in front of your eyes. The glory of Lothlorien is but a mere hint at the
ancient kingdom of old. Someday, even this realm will be gone, as the prophecy
foretells.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
This little clearing in the Golden Wood opens up to a small mound covered by the
lush, green grass and the forest flowers. Tall, golden stalks of some flowering
plant surround the mound. The aroma of many blossoms pervades this area, but
does not overpower.
name: A Clearing in the Forest

desc:
For some reason, a circle of white stones gathered from the river has been
constructed here. Whether it was designed as a spot for meditation among the
beautiful mellyrn of this forest, or was simply the product of a child's
afternoon activity, it is hard to determine.
name: A Circle of Stones

desc:
The mallorn trees begin to give way to sunlight to the east as the ground nears
the edge of a small cleft. From here it is impossible to see how far the drop
off is. With the increased sunlight in this area, various types of scrub brush
and other low-lying plants have been able to flourish. The colours of their
flowers engage the eye. Pastel blues, reds, and oranges mix with the abundant
gold and silver of the mallorn trees, making the sight a feast for the eyes.
name: Forest Nearing a Cliff

desc:
The forest continues down a slight grade towards the east. One can see the
mellyrn of various heights that stand to the east. The reddish-orange sunrise
over the golden tree-tops must make a magnificent sight. All over the forest
floor divots can be seen, indicating that some forest creatures have been
foraging for food here.
name: Rolling Forest

desc:
A wooden flet is secured among the lower branches of a tall mallorn tree. Dry
branches that form its floor are drawn together with pieces of silvery bark. The
whole of it is secured to the wider branches of the supporting tree using four
lengths of luminous elven rope. The ends of the ladder are made of the same rope
and tied around the main trunk. A few smaller flets are visible in the branches
of adjacent trees. They look as if they might be accessible from here.
name: Border Flet

desc:
This is the home of an elven family. The talan, polished smooth, is of medium
size, enough to house a few elves and their possessions. The furniture is simple
and unmistakably of elven make. The only entrance into this home is the rope
ladder leading up the trunk of the great tree.
name: An Elven Home

desc:
After ascending the fine threaded rope ladder to this talan, a wondrous room is
revealed. So neatly tucked away in the higher parts of the mallorn tree is this
place, surrounded by a thin screen coloured in pale greens and yellows, that one
could very easily miss it. The talan itself is quite large and is populated with
well-polished wooden furniture, tapestries displayed over the railings and fine
embroidered rugs on the smooth wooden floor.
name: Large Talan

desc:
This talan rests high in the branches of a mallorn. It seems that this part of
the tree serves as the comfortable bedroom for the elves who live here. On the
floor are several wooden pallets with soft down mattresses on them. This talan
is not as large as the lower one, but is still a good size, with room enough for
a family of elves.
name: Small Talan

desc:
Quiet and solemn, the tall trees stand here reaching upward, as if straining to
reach the sky in competition with the green wall encircling the city. A rope is
tied to several trees and poles forming an enclosure. The enclosed area contains
an archery range with distinct shooting stations. The slope is rather level here
but rises quite sharply to the northeast, climbing towards the crown of the
green hill.
name: The Golden Forest

desc:
On the western slope of the hill close to the wall stand a few trees that are
not of mallorn kind. Large and fair, these apple trees strive to match the
splendour of the golden forest, but struggle in vain. Even bearing some of the
finest apples known on Arda, the poor trees shrink in comparison to the
surrounding woods, but even so they are loved and cared for by the elves of
Caras Galadhon, who gratefully accept the fruit of the competition.
name: An Orchard

desc:
Ascent of the thin yet strong rope ladder affords access to one of the many
flets, or talans in the elvish tongue, that populate the forest of Lothlorien.
This flet seems to be used primarily for storage. It is made of the strongest
wood, smoothly polished by countless footsteps. You can see a bridge over the
stream to the north, and the stepping stones over the stream to the east.
name: Atop a Flet

desc:
A marvellous view opens from the top of the green and broad wall. Circling the
city, the broad and high defensive line can be easily defended against any foe
that has managed to reach the capital of Lothlorien. The vast forest, reaching
towards the hazy horizon, lies at your feet seemingly within your reach. Even
closer by, the encircling stone road runs in a perfect semi-circle just outside
the deep fosse that lies at the foot of the wall.
name: On the Wall

desc:
Curving slightly, the wall here faces almost due west giving one a nice if not
to say a splendid view of the white stone road that encircles the city. Just
outside a deep shadowed fosse lies the perfect semi-circle of carefully placed
white stones as a border between the city and the forest. The tall trees
standing to the side of the road seem content with their place as the road runs
undisturbed and unmarked by the forces of nature.
name: Western Wall

desc:
High and broad, this strong wall gives ample room for several archers to hold
here a defensive position. Facing almost due west, one could see what moves
below on the white stone road that encircles the city or, for that matter,
anything that comes out of the vast forest.
name: Western Wall

desc:
Positioned on the great defensive walls of the fair city of Caras Galadhon, you
find yourself high above the ground, thus having an excellent view. Large
crenellations are placed along the wall, crafted in such a way as to blend in
with the wall and the surrounding greenery, and allowing a defender safely to
fire his arrows at an incoming foe. Below lies the deep fosse in permanent dusk.
Farther out, the white stone road encircles the city and beyond it the vast
expanse of the golden forest stretches out endlessly.
name: Western Wall

desc:
The small clearing close to the side of the path lies quiet and tranquil just
below the great green city wall. Surrounded by tall trees, it cannot be easily
found unless one knows the way around. When the light of the skies shines down
upon the grass on the western slope of the hill, a dazzling display of shadows
dances over the greenery to the tune of singing birds.
name: A Tranquil Glade

desc:
By the foot of the western slope, the wall surrounding the city towers high
above you, only to be challenged by the tall mellyrn standing nearby. The trees
grow densely here, undisturbed by will of elves. A short distance to the north
the forest opens up where a small but carefully nurtured orchard thrives in the
rich soil.
name: The Golden Forest

desc:
The floor of this flet is more narrow than wide and there is scarcely more than
crouching room left here for elf or man. Racks of arrows are secured to the
boles of the two mellyrn whose branches support this platform. Through the dense
canopy of the branches one can catch a glimpse of the shimmering waters of the
river below and the dusky gloom of the forest on the other side of it.
name: Elven Archers' Flet

desc:
The lower branches of the tallest and mightiest mallorn tree around support this
flet. It is secured at about the third of the way up the trunk. The lower part
of the trunk is too bare to climb but bears enough young growth to conceal this
flet from the prying eyes below. The branches part slightly to the south,
providing a vantage point to observe traffic along Celebrant.
name: Observation Perch

desc:
The forest suddenly opens up into an enchanting glade, ringed with mellyrn, but
open to the vaulted sky. Underneath the forest floor is carpeted with grass and
mosses of the softest and lushest nature, in the centre is a startlingly wide
stump. The surface of the stump has been smoothed and polished, then coated with
some sort of clear resin creating a glassy sheen on its surface. A good dozen
people perhaps could be seated about it with no fear of crowding, though the
soft matting of grass must suffice for a seat. Glistening spider webs lace the
trees with dewdrops hanging from them like diamond adornments.
name: An Enchanting Glade

desc:
Aspiring to heights above all others, a tremendously large and aged mallorn
springs from the soil here. It is as close as could be to an aged mallorn, as
its bark has begun to show the faintest signs of cracking and dullness.
Nevertheless, the grand old tree radiates no sense of decay or weakness, to the
contrary, it shows strength and prominence beyond that of other trees.
name: At a Venerable Mallorn

desc:
Sparkling boughs interweave their limbs above, coalescing into faintly fluted
trunks of glistening silver rooted firmly in the rich loam of the forest floor.
Essences of fragrances found not in mortal lands stimulate the senses, and vague
song-like whisperings echo amid the trees.
name: Under Silver Boughs

desc:
Here, the ever-serene trees grow denser and denser together. Their branches and
boughs embrace each other, and in doing so they create an almost perfect
circle-shaped enclosure with only one exit: toward the south, where the
silver-laden forest continues.
name: Forest Glen

desc:
Spartan, yet comfortable, this flet undoubtedly stays warm and dry even in the
fiercest of storms. Furnishings are limited to a sturdy desk and chair of
crafted wood, some wooden cabinets treated with simple, elegant, inlaid carvings
and a comfy bed. It stands high indeed, perched as far towards the sky as the
ancient tree that holds it affords; one can survey the clear aqua gleam of the
rivers to the west, framed by the hazy outlines of the mountains towering above.
To the east and south stretch the vast glades of Lorien.
name: A Cosy Flet

desc:
This narrow tunnel slides down into the darkness at a very steep angle, its
sides slick and smooth as if constructed with great skill. The sides are lightly
grooved, as if a master mason had spent countless hours working in this most
unhospitable place.
name: A Smoothly-walled Chute

desc:
A few large stones lie in the water, making it possible to cross the stream
without getting the feet wet. Even though the trail is well travelled, the light
steps of the Elves of this wood have not worn away the mossy coating on the
stones. A few mallorn branches were caught among the stones and lie in the
current, glistening silvery as the sparkling stream rolls across them.
name: Stones across the Stream

desc:
Deep inside the mallorn crown and high up the trunk a wooden platform encircles
this ancient tree. Hardly two hand-spans wide, the flet is placed precariously
among the weaker tree-top branches, its only support. A truly breath-taking view
opens to the south of here. Another smaller flet can be distinguished far down
the tree from here.
name: Crown Talan Entrance

desc:
Deep inside the mallorn crown and high up the trunk a wooden platform encircles
this ancient tree. Hardly two hand-spans wide, the flet is placed precariously
among the weaker tree-top branches, its only support. The Misty Mountains raise
threateningly to the west and north, barring the view of Eregion and other lands
beyond.
name: The Crown Talan

desc:
Deep inside the mallorn crown and high up the trunk a wooden platform encircles
this ancient tree. Hardly two hand-spans wide, the flet is placed precariously
among the weaker tree-top branches, its only support. A spectacular view opens
south and east from here onto the empty lands beyond the rivers.
name: The Crown Talan

desc:
Deep inside the mallorn crown and high up the trunk a wooden platform encircles
this ancient tree. Hardly two hand-spans wide, the flet is placed precariously
among the weaker tree-top branches, its only support. The golden forest
stretches far and wide, and this perch provides one a view even beyond the
northern and eastern borders of Lorien.
name: The Crown Talan

desc:
A natural grotto opens up here, with protruding stalactites and stalagmites
clustered in rows like a huge gaping maw of sharp, dripping fangs. Moisture
dripping down the stalactites makes an eerie echo as it drips into clear puddles
and pools. There is a tunnel slanting back up to the surface, but it is smooth
as glass, and very steep; a climb fit only for the most skilled. Several narrow,
twisting, tube-like tunnels have been somehow bored into the solid rock by some
manner which you cannot divine.
name: An Open Cavern

desc:
Shallow puddles of water lie in broad pools across most of the floor of this
cavern. The air is moist and thick. The dripping of water from the ceiling
creates a continual string of soft echoes that combine with each other to form
an effect that is both soothing and oddly disconcerting.
name: An Open Cavern

desc:
A single huge stalagmite dominates the centre of this large, fairly circular
cavern, flanked by a retinue of smaller stalagmites. The overall effect of this
formation suggests some sort of intelligent design, but more likely it is merely
a matter of randomness upon occasion falling into patterns that suggest a
pattern to one's conscious mind.
name: An Open Cavern

desc:
This irregularly shaped cavern is wide and has a low undulating roof, which
rises only about ten feet above the floor. The surrounding rock is the same
pale, sometimes greenish limestone that forms the rest of this cavern network.
name: An Open Cavern

desc:
The rough limestone walls of the cavern have been smoothed somewhat by the
steady action of water seeping from the ceiling and sides of the cave. There are
fewer stalactites here, but some blunted spires and nodules have been formed
from the depositing of dissolved sediments.
name: An Open Cavern

desc:
The tunnel opens up into startling sight: a grotto embellished with a plethora
of glittering stalactites hung like fairy-tale icicles in pastoral shades. The
whole scene seems out of place in this otherwise foreboding environ, but
nonetheless the enchantment of the place is palpable.
name: An Open Cavern

desc:
This entire cavern is suffused with a powerful, dank odour emanating strongly
from several piles of heaped debris in one corner. Untidy piles of broken bones
line the damp, mould-infested limestone walls. The ceiling is low, with short,
sharp stalactites viciously threatening to scrape any unprotected scalp.
name: A Foetid Lair

desc:
Far beneath the earth, this damp cave has smooth limestone walls glistening
faintly with embedded flecks of mica. An icy-cold pool of black water dominates
the centre of the cave. From an undetermined direction comes the faint sound of
rapidly rushing water.
name: Deep beneath the Earth
